clean_text: ط بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم الحمد لله والصلاه والسلام على رسول الله اما بعد يقول الحافظ النووي في رياضه في باب التوبه في سياق حديث ابي سعيد الخدري الطويل في ذكر قاتل ال 99 نفسا ‏in ابو سعيد الخدري رضي الله عنه which is repentance which is often what is most looked ‏other manur 99 souls taken 99 lives and s out the knowledable ‏available to ask him whether or not he was eligible for repentance if he could repent after such atrocities and he was directed to a monk a person who in all for all intents and purposes was a righteous individual someone who worships Allah subhanahu waala ‏ofous and uping that was this man was to him however this monk made a fatal mistake not just for himself not just for this man but also for society at large. لما نذكر اشد الخطر على الانسان ما هو الذي يضاد العلم المضاداه التامه هل هو الجاهل لا الجهل هو داء صحيح داء قاتل لكن له شفاء شفاؤه السؤال كما قال النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم انما شفاء العي السؤال هكذا قال شفاء العي الجهل المشكله الجهل المركب ما هو الجهل المركب يقول الاصوليون الجهل المركب هو او على عباره العلماء هو الجاهل الذي يجهل انه جاهل فاي شفاء لهذا الجهل البسيط عند الاصوليين ‏ised rather easily knowledge ignorance the real enemy to knowledge is compounded ignorance what is compounded ignorance compounded ignorance is a person who is ignorant but is unaware that he is ignorant he is ignorant but is deluded into thinking that he knows this is ‏Why? Because how can you some impossible it's a upside down and putting it under the under the to get a glass of water. How can you fill it up? It's impossible. And so this man was not just ignorant but he was suffering of compounded ignorance. ‏Because if was regular when the man asked him I have killed 99 kill 99 people can repent would I don't know this answered unit ‏and if we understand the true fall of compounded ignorance this send a little shiver up our spine because not just of the ignorance or of the compounded nature of this ignorance but the confidence that a person of compounded ignorance has. ‏spe لا فقتله فكمل به المئه صلى الله عليه وسلم ثم سال عن اعلم اهل الارض فدل على رجل عالم فقال انه قتل 100 نفس فهل له من توبه فقال نعم ومن يحول بينه وبين التوبه ‏And then the man bad again and consci wasing him again and so gave it another shot he asked again about the knowledable person on now the sitation is worse than it was before for all parties involved and this time he was directed to a schar and he came to him and asked him he is killed ‏could answer yes of what stand between you andent and as will he in another ofent ‏and that will neveros بها اناساون فاعبد الله معهم ولا ترجع الى ارضك فانها ارض سوء and him also him detailed instructions as to how to go about his repentance he said go to such and such land a town or city or state or whever it was go to such and such land for indeed there are people الله نسال الله سبحانه وتعالى بمنه وكرمه وجوده ان يتقبل منا توباتنا وان يغسل حوباتنا وان يسلل سخيمه قلوبنا اللهم امين وصل اللهم وسلم على نبينا محمد وعلى اله واصحابه اجمعين.

summary: This lecture discusses the famous hadith from Riyadul Saliheen about a man who killed 99 souls and sought repentance. The scholar emphasizes the critical difference between simple ignorance and compounded ignorance (جهل مركب), using the story to illustrate how a righteous-appearing monk's lack of knowledge led to tragic consequences. The monk, suffering from compounded ignorance, confidently gave wrong advice, while a true scholar later provided correct guidance on repentance and directed the man to a righteous community for proper reformation.
suggested_title: Compounded Ignorance and the Story of Repentance: Insights from Riyadul Saliheen
catchy_title: When Worship Isn't Enough: The Danger of Ignorance
